crisis_name: Student-worker uprising and transfer from Tsiranana to Ramanantsoa
claim_summary: Student-worker mobilization framed the crisis as a rejection of the established order and postcolonial dependence.
textual_evidence: Student-worker mobilization framed the crisis as a rejection of the established order and postcolonial dependence.
people_invocation: popular/youth uprising against the established order
keywords: youth; students; workers; people; anti-neocolonial order; street
settlement_context: Transfer of full powers from Philibert Tsiranana to General Gabriel Ramanantsoa; 1972 referendum and Loi constitutionnelle provisoire; Revised Franco-Malagasy cooperation accords; Tsiranana handed full powers to General Ramanantsoa to form a government and resolve the 1972 crisis. This was not a negotiated multiparty accord but functioned as the decisive executive settlement.; Popular vote confirmed Ramanantsoa's five-year transition and was followed by the provisional constitutional law of 7 November 1972, replacing the 1959 constitutional order during transition.; Renegotiated the 1960 cooperation regime after the 1972 rupture, including French military/strategic and cooperation questions.
constitutional_context: E-1972-01; substitutes for 1959 Constitution and legalizes military-led provisional government; formalized_transition_or_exception
